title: gilia austrooccidentalis
intro: Gilia austrooccidentalis, also known as Sky Pilot, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits such as soothing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Gilia austrooccidentalis is often prepared as a tea or tincture for herbal use, with the roots and leaves being the primary sources of the medicinal compounds.
